title: Typha-Dor

{{Quote|Typha-Dor is lucky ... We are rich in resources. We have abundant sunshine and water. Our world is large and varied. We have a large workforce. Yet we have never learned how to truly manage our resources and turn them into the wealth we need ... I am not defending Vanqor's aggression ... I am just saying that even Vanqor could have lessons to teach us.|Thik, to his fellow TY44 surveillance outpost crew members.|<ref name="JQ7" />}}
'''Typha-Dor''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Uziel system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

The planet was rich in natural resources, with abundant sunshine and water, and had a "string of [[Moon/Legends|moons]]," one of which was [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] [[TY44]], so named because of its coordinates.<ref name="JQ7" />

Large and varied, Typha-Dor had twin capital cities, [[Sarus-Dor]] and [[Ith-Dor]], two rulers, and a substantial workforce. Even so, the government's [[Typha-Dor High Council|High Council]] had never managed to learn how to truly manage and convert the planet's resources into the wealth it needed.

In [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]], thanks to the mediation efforts of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who [[Mission to Typha-Dor|were sent]] by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] at the request of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], Typha-Dor—under its "twin rulers" [[Binalu]] and [[Talus (Human)|Talus]]—established a ceasefire with its longtime rival [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]], a neighboring world that had attempted to invade and conquer it.
